etymology.description: Phonetic: 臼(臼) (jiù, "mortar") + Semantic: 男(男) (nán, "male") = uncle
etymology.interpretation: The character 舅 combines the phonetic component 臼(臼) (jiù, "mortar"), which suggests the sound of the word, with the semantic component 男(男) (nán, "male"), indicating its meaning as a male relative, specifically the mother's brother.

examples.chinese: 我舅今天来访(我舅今天來訪)。
我去拜访(我去拜訪)，舅家的人热情招待(舅家的人熱情招待)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ qù bài fǎng, jiù jiā de rén rè qíng zhāo dài.
examples.english: I went to visit, and my uncle's family warmly welcomed me.

examples.chinese: 舅喜欢讲故事(舅喜歡講故事)。
